intro: Urochloa lachnantha, also known as Urochloa grass, is a tropical grass species native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Urochloa lachnantha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Urochloa lachnantha is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat various health conditions, or as a paste or salve to topically apply its medicinal properties.
